schema.appName: Post Office TD Calculator — Time Deposit
schema.appDescription: Calculate Post Office Time Deposit (FD) maturity value. Rates: 1yr 6.9%, 2yr 7.0%, 3yr 7.1%, 5yr 7.5%. 5-year TD qualifies for Section 80C.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Post Office TD Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown table to see how your numbers change over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust the inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option.

faq.q: What are Post Office Time Deposit rates for 2026?
faq.a: Q1 FY 2026-27 rates: 1 year TD: 6.9% p.a., 2 year: 7.0%, 3 year: 7.1%, 5 year: 7.5%. Interest is compounded quarterly but paid annually. The 5-year TD qualifies for Section 80C deduction up to ₹1.5 lakh.

faq.q: How is Post Office TD different from bank FD?
faq.a: Post Office TD is backed by the Government of India (sovereign guarantee), while bank FDs are insured only up to ₹5 lakh per bank under DICGC. PO TD rates are typically competitive with top bank FD rates. TDS is not deducted on POTD interest.

faq.q: Can PO TD be broken prematurely?
faq.a: Premature withdrawal is allowed after 6 months at simple interest rate. After 1 year, 2% deduction from applicable TD rate. The original rate is restored if held to maturity.

faq.q: Is the Post Office TD Calculator free?
faq.a: Yes, the Post Office TD Calculator on CalcPhi is completely free with no registration required.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We do not store any financial data.

faq.q: Can I use the Post Office TD Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi is fully mobile-optimised and works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹1 lakh 5-year Post Office TD at 7.5%
examples.text: ₹1 lakh in 5-year PO TD at 7.5% p.a. (quarterly compounding) yields ₹1,44,803 at maturity. Interest earned: ₹44,803. The deposit qualifies for Section 80C deduction, effectively reducing the cost for those in the 30% bracket.

examples.heading: Comparison: PO TD vs Bank FD vs NSC
examples.text: For ₹2 lakh, 5-year investments: PO TD 7.5% → ₹2,89,606; NSC 7.7% → ₹2,89,806 (similar, plus 80C); SBI FD at 6.5% → ₹2,73,749. Post Office schemes are typically competitive with government guarantee.
